response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack software developer with a strong web, backend, and testing background, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Aside from some low-latency systems work in DLang and brief C/C++ tooling experience, there is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture, or silicon design collaboration. The domain mismatch is fundamental rather than marginal.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, exceeding the years-of-experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career centered on web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware-software co-design scope

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator expertise
No deep-learning or GPU workload optimization background
Strong general engineering seniority and systems experience
